ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Yesu ɔnɔ nnɛ mato maɣɛ, ne ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Manyɛse i kuabaradze ɔnya ne iiɖe mma nsarɛ.
translated: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child will never enter it.”

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: Nto loɣere mi sɔ maturi gbodzoo to maabɔrɛ i kuɣɛ̃kabɔrɛkɔ̃ gu kɔ̃ iyo kabokɔ̃ maba maasɛ i ɔpɔrɔ̃ katɔ̃ maɖe ara ku Abraham gu Isak gu Yakob i Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃.
translated: They are the ancestors who were given the land from the beginning, from the time of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob until now.

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu iiɣɛ kuira. Ne ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maaɣere wũ sɔ, “Sa wũ si ɔnyua kulu ɔla ɔtɛ̃ra bo atsue!”
translated: Yesu said to them, "This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent."

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Si kuɣɛ̃ to kusɛ kuabo iyo fiɛ kuɖokpo ɔrɛtɛ ne, misɛ miɣɛ sɔ, ‘Kayi to kaakpa kɔrãkaɖe.’
translated: Yesu said to them, “Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me.

ref: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
text: Fiɛ si ɔnya wũ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ ɔ̃ana isoɣɔ i ɔwɛ̃ gɔ loyu ngbe iso ɔɖo kasiwɛ̃-kaiwɛ̃ ma loiyu.
translated: This is what I heard, they said that I should not go because they are afraid that I will go and get lost in the bush and not return.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Gɔ Herodes ɔkpi ne, Ɣaa kpabo ɔwɛ̃ ɔɖi so ɔte Yosef i kurɛ ame i Egipte.
translated: When Herod heard this, he sent messengers to kill the children in Bethlehem and in all that region.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Be ɔso mito mimararã so i nnɛ miapia iso? Minyɔ ipo ame adziri kumɛgɔ asɛ amɔ. Aisɛ ata ɖɔti ɣee awe sikati.
translated: “What is the reason why you are always angry? It is because you are not satisfied with what you have. If you are content with what you have, you will be happy.”

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mi ma lonyanyarĩ lɛ maɣɛ! Nda miabara fiɛ miawo ara sɛɛ ɔɣɛ gɔ miɖe maturi lalaa? Alasɔ nnɛ loyi ɔturi ɔtu ame i kanya sɛ kaɣɛ.
translated: What are you doing? Why are you looking at me like that? Don't you know that I'm a woman?

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Ne ɔso si ɔrere ɔba ɔ̃aɣere mi sɔ, ‘Nyɔ, ɔɔnya Kristo i ngbe,’ ɣee ɔrere ɔɣere mi sɔ, ‘Ɔpia i mmɔ’ ne, midaafɔ miɖe.
translated: He said to me, 'Yes, I know Christ,' and then he said to me, 'He is my brother,' and I was happy.

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Ne Petro ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Lokã sɔ ɔnukuare nto loɣɛ! Ɣaa si ɔkpadzɛ̃ me kutsue si iiɖe gbaã iɖe sɔ loiɣe ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔmɔ!” Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, kɔkɔ ɔɖi kanya.
translated: Peter said, "Look! The children are playing in the water! They are splashing each other and laughing." He smiled and went inside.

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: Misɛ miɣɛ sɔ, ‘Si bo nsi ngbã ku bo mawa ɔwi ne, tee boiɖoe kanyamaɖidze mamɔ lɛ bo mawa awe.’
translated: If you want to know, 'If someone tells you that he is going to give you something, but he doesn't give it to you, then he is a liar.'

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Ɔ̃ itɔ̃me ɔɖo Yesu kayiri, ne ɔso ɔɖi kɔrɔ̃ ɔpɛgu wũ. Ne ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Loɖɔɛ, se sekelee!”
translated: When Jesus saw them, he was moved with compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd. Then he said to his disciples, "The harvest truly is plentiful, but the laborers are few."

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu ɔma kanya kpa. Ne sɔrɛdzekpakpa pelepele ɔledza iɣɛ sɔ, “I Ɣaa iyere ame ne, nto lokã ndamu lotã-ɔ sɔ, ɣere bo si fɔ nɖe Kristo gɔ nɖe Ɣaa Ɔbi.”
translated: Yesu said to them, "Truly I say to you, whoever believes in me, though he die, will live. And whoever lives and believes in me will never die."

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Gɔ ɔɣɛ ngbɔ ne, sɔrɛdzekpakpa pelepele ɔkɛ ɔ̃ awu ne ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Mmusuo! Maɖansiɛ kumawɛ̃ iisinya bo. Mi ɔɖuɖu ɔnɔ mmusuo itɔ̃me nɛ ɔɣɛ!
translated: Woman, you are beautiful, your skin is smooth and shiny. I am a man, I am looking for a wife, and I want to marry you!

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Yesu ɔbore ɔbo ma ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa ɔsu ɔle ɔɖuɖu gɔ i kato gu karɔ̃ ɔtã me.
translated: Jesus said to them, "Truly I say to you, whoever believes in me will also do the works that I do; and greater works than these will he do, because I go to the Father."

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Gɔ Herodes ɔkpi ne, Ɣaa kpabo ɔwɛ̃ ɔɖi so ɔte Yosef i kurɛ ame i Egipte.
translated: When Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, "Get up, take the child and his mother and go to Egypt."

translated: “Are you the one who will help us carry this heavy load? We are tired and our strength is failing. We need your help to carry this burden.”

translated: Why are you so quiet? Don't you want to know what happened to the young man who went to the forest? He was eaten by a lion.

translated: He said to me, 'No, you must accept Christ,' then he said to me, 'He is the truth,' and I was convinced.

translated: Peter said, “Look! The wild animals are coming! Quickly, everyone, run to the house and hide!” And they all ran quickly.

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Ne ɔso Yesu ɔbore ɔbo wũ, ɔmɔɛ̃ wũ kɔrɔ̃ ɔtara wũ ɔɣedza. Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔsarɛ ɔta ɔwa aɖera ɔtã ma.
translated: When Jesus was born, the angels sang and rejoiced. God sent his only son to save us.

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Katɔ̃memasɛdze ma i Yesu ɔsese sɔ masɛ maaki maɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea ɔkpese maba maaɣere wũ nwa ɔɖuɖu mabara gu nwa masɛ maate i ma irisɛ ame.
translated: The people were amazed at Jesus because he healed the sick. They said, "This man is the prophet who is to come into the world."

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
text: Maina ɔri mabo i iyo alasɔ maturi ɔɣede. Ne ɔso ne, masɛ maabusi isã makigu nyɛse masora wũ i ɔkala ame masɛ i Yesu katɔ̃.
translated: Maina said that he would not go to the market because he was sick. He said that he was very weak and could not go to the market to buy food for his family.

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yesu iifɔ itɔ̃me ne maɣɛ mmɔ. Ne ɔɣere Yairo sɔ, “Daanigã, fɔɖe kere!”
translated: Jesus went with him. He came to Jairus' house and said, "Don't be afraid."

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: Nto loɣere mi gbaã sɔ loisibanɛ ndã gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ iyi nɛ loanɛgu mi miɣɛtɛ i wũ Ɔse sigarakaɖekɔ̃.”
translated: I went to the market to buy some fish, but I didn't find any good ones, so I came back home.
